normalization: trade value converted from thousand USD to million USD and divided by maximum retained flow value per instance

cost_operator.operator_form: F[k,i,j,l] = a_cost[k,i]*(1 + b_congestion*sum_{j,l} x[k,i,j,l]) + tau_transport*a_cost[k,i] - p_price[k,j]/(1 + d_demand[k,j]*sum_{i,l} x[k,i,j,l])
cost_operator.fixed_defaults_note: b_congestion and tau_transport are reproducible benchmark defaults, not structurally estimated parameters.

calibration_formulas_used_by_code.avg_cost[k,j]: mean over active exporters i with x_obs[k,i,j,:].sum()>0 of a_cost[k,i]*(1+b_congestion*export_total[k,i]) + tau_transport*a_cost[k,i]; otherwise mean_i a_cost[k,i]

feasible_set: nonnegative flows with exporter-wise capacity sum_{k,j,l} x[k,i,j,l] <= supply_cap[i]

sector_policy.year_level_country_selection: Top 10 exporters and top 10 importers are selected independently for each year by aggregate bilateral trade value.
sector_policy.year_level_sector_order: Fixed canonical order in NPZ files after running run_pipeline.py.
sector_policy.sector_level_scope: Five single-sector instances are generated for year 2022.

operator_properties.lipschitz_continuity: F is Lipschitz continuous on compact C under the stored finite parameter arrays.
operator_properties.pseudomonotonicity_note: The instances are intended as pseudomonotone network-equilibrium benchmarks; strict monotonicity and uniqueness are not guaranteed.
operator_properties.reference_solution_note: Pre-computed high-accuracy reference solutions are not stored in v0.3; users may approximate them with included solvers and a tight residual tolerance such as G(x)<1e-8.
